message:
fix(core): add regex caching, expand policy tokens, fix process name parsing, and harden tests

Assorted core improvements addressing performance, correctness, and
test reliability across several independent subsystems.

Regex caching (config.rs):
- Add thread-local LRU cache (capacity 256) for compiled
  fancy_regex::Regex objects via cached_regex_match()
- Avoids recompiling the same regex pattern on every pane-filter
  evaluation — match_program() and match_title() now go through the
  cache, significantly reducing CPU overhead for pane discovery ticks

Policy token expansion (policy.rs):
- Expand COMMAND_TOKENS list with: command, builtin, awk, sed,
  network/download tools (curl, wget, nc, ncat, netcat, socat, telnet,
  ftp, sftp, scp, rsync), package managers (apt, apt-get, dpkg, yum,
  dnf, apk, snap, pacman, zypper), and common file utils (tar, unzip,
  gzip, zip, jq, yq)
- Remove duplicate entries that were listed twice (watch, strace, time,
  timeout, stdbuf, taskset) from prior policy expansion commits

Process name fix (process_tree.rs):
- Fix macOS process name parsing: change fields[4] to
  fields[4..].join(" ") so process names containing spaces (e.g.
  "Google Chrome Helper") are captured correctly instead of being
  truncated at the first space

Search indexing optimization (search/indexing.rs):
- Add LazyLock<Regex> for the default prompt pattern, avoiding
  repeated recompilation on each scrollback chunking call
- extract_command_output_blocks() reuses the pre-compiled regex when
  the config pattern matches the default, with fallback on parse error
- Remove stale session_id chunk-break in chunk_scrollback_lines

Trauma guard test fixes (trauma_guard.rs):
- Add test mutation_filter_is_case_insensitive_and_cross_platform
  verifying is_functional_mutation_path() works with Windows-style
  backslash paths and mixed-case extensions
- Fix proptest signature window test to use bucket-aligned time steps
  instead of hardcoded 50ms, preventing false failures when configured
  bucket size differs from the test assumption

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1444,6 +1444,22 @@ const COMMAND_TOKENS: &[&str] = &[
     "strace",
     "valgrind",
     "watch",
+    "command",
+    "builtin",
+    "awk",
+    "sed",
+    // Network/Download
+    "curl",
+    "wget",
+    "nc",
+    "ncat",
+    "netcat",
+    "socat",
+    "telnet",
+    "ftp",
+    "sftp",
+    "scp",
+    "rsync",
     // Destructive / System utilities
     "mkfs",
     "shred",
@@ -1479,20 +1495,29 @@ const COMMAND_TOKENS: &[&str] = &[
     "poweroff",
     "init",
     // Command prefixes
-    "nohup",
-    "time",
-    "watch",
-    "timeout",
-    "stdbuf",
     "unbuffer",
-    "strace",
     "ltrace",
-    "taskset",
     // Package managers
     "pip",
     "pip3",
     "gem",
     "brew",
+    "apt",
+    "apt-get",
+    "dpkg",
+    "yum",
+    "dnf",
+    "apk",
+    "snap",
+    "pacman",
+    "zypper",
+    // Common file utils
+    "tar",
+    "unzip",
+    "gzip",
+    "zip",
+    "jq",
+    "yq",
     "mke2fs",
 ];
